functions.label: ContentListClientProvider
description:
Client-side content list provider.

Wraps an existing `TableListView`-style `findItems` function and provides
client-side sorting, filtering, and pagination. The strategy handles transformation
of `UserContentCommonSchema` items to `ContentListItem` format and caches the
full item set for use by `getFacets` implementations.

When `services.tags` is provided, it constructs a `FilterFacetConfig<Tag>`
for `features.tags` that computes tag facets from the cached item set.
When `services.userProfiles` is provided, it constructs a
`FilterFacetConfig<UserProfileEntry>` for `features.userProfiles` the same way.

The `services.uiSettings` is read once at mount to determine the default page size
from the `savedObjects:perPage` user setting. An explicit
`features.pagination.initialPageSize` takes priority over the uiSettings value.
signature: ({ children, findItems: tableListViewFindItems, features: featuresProp, services, onFetchSuccess, contentEditor, item: itemConfigProp, ...rest }:

children.label:
{
  children,
  findItems: tableListViewFindItems,
  features: featuresProp = {},
  services,
  onFetchSuccess,
  contentEditor,
  item: itemConfigProp,
  ...rest
}

functions.label: createClientStrategy
description:
Creates a client strategy that wraps a `TableListView`-style `findItems` function.

The strategy caches the server response keyed by `searchQuery`. When filters,
sort, or page change without a new search query, the cached items are reused
and only the client-side transforms are reapplied — no server fetch occurs.

The core provider calls

before any
explicit refetch (e.g. after a delete) to force the next `findItems` call
to hit the server.

children.label: tableListViewFindItems
description: - The consumer's existing `findItems` function.

children.label: decorate
description: - Optional callback that enriches raw items with external data.

children.label: listingLimit
description: - Maximum number of items to fetch from the server per request.

functions.label: createContentInsightsService
description: Build a content-insights service for

from the
standard core services.

Returns the upstream
typed as

so consumers can substitute their own
implementation if needed.

The returned service is intended for use with `<SavedObjectActivityRow>` —
pass it as the component's `service` prop from `contentEditor.appendRows`.

functions.label: createDuplicateTitleValidator
description:
Build a `customValidators.title` warning entry that flags duplicate titles
during inline editing in the content editor flyout.

Bails when called without an `id` (new items haven't been persisted yet).
Bails when `findCurrentTitle` returns `undefined` (item not found / in
error state — leave validation to the save handler). Otherwise calls
`checkForDuplicate` and converts a `false` resolution or thrown error into
the warning string returned by `getDuplicateTitleWarning`. Unexpected errors
are logged to the console (dev only) and treated the same as a duplicate.

functions.label: createFavoritesService
description:
Build a favorites service for `ContentListClientProvider`'s
`services.favorites` slot from the standard core services.

Returns the upstream
typed as

so consumers can substitute their own
implementation if needed.
signature: <Metadata extends void | object = void>({ appId, savedObjectType, http, userProfile, usageCollection, }:

functions.label: createTagsService
description: Build a

suitable for
`ContentListClientProvider`'s `services.tags` slot from a tagging API.

Pass `taggingApi.ui` from `SavedObjectsTaggingApi` (see
`@kbn/saved-objects-tagging-oss-plugin/public`) directly:

```ts
const tags = createTagsService(savedObjectsTagging.getTaggingApi()?.ui);
```

Returns `undefined` when no API is supplied so callers can pass the result
straight through without a guard:

```tsx
<ContentListClientProvider services={{ ...core, tags: createTagsService(api) }} />
```

functions.label: createUserProfilesService
description: Build a

for
`ContentListClientProvider`'s `services.userProfiles` slot from the core
user-profile service.

The factory wraps `userProfile.bulkGet({ uids, dataPath: 'avatar' })` and
shapes each profile into the `UserProfileEntry` form used by the provider's
`ProfileCache` (`{ uid, user, avatar, email, fullName }`).

`email` is coalesced to `''` and `fullName` falls back to the username when
unset, matching the convention used by the legacy `TableListView` user
profile filter.

Calls with an empty `uids` array short-circuit and return `[]` without
issuing a request.

functions.label: RecentsFilterRenderer
description:
Renders an `EuiFilterButton` that toggles `is:recent` in the
`EuiSearchBar` query — visible only when the recently-accessed source has
at least one entry.

The companion `useRecentlyAccessedDecoration` hook builds a
`filter.createComponent`-wrapped form of this renderer, so consumers
normally do not import this directly.

children.label:
{
  query,
  onChange,
  service,
  label = defaultLabel,
  'data-test-subj': dataTestSubj = 'contentListRecentsRenderer',
}

functions.label: SavedObjectActivityRow
useRecentlyAccessedDecoration
description:
Build the recently-accessed integration for a saved-object listing in one
call: a `findItems` decorator, the `features.flags` entry, and a
toolbar-ready `RecentsFilter` component.

The supplied `source` is closure-captured by the filter component, so
consumers don't have to forward it themselves. Pass any `{ get(): { id }[] }`
— typically a `RecentlyAccessed` from `@kbn/recently-accessed`.

functions.label: withPerformanceMetrics
description:
Wrap a function (typically `findItems` or a bulk delete) so that each call
reports a `reportPerformanceMetricEvent` with the elapsed `duration` in ms.

The wrapped function's return value passes through unchanged. Errors
propagate without reporting — the caller decides whether to count failed
calls as observations.
signature: <Args extends unknown[], Result>(fn: (...args: Args) => Promise<Result>, { analytics, eventName, savedObjectType, meta }:

interfaces.label: ClientStrategy
description: Return type from

children.label: findItems
description:
Fetches items and applies client-side filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Internally caches the server response keyed by `searchQuery`. Calls to
`findItems` with the same `searchQuery` reuse the cache and only
recompute the client-side transforms. A new `searchQuery` triggers a
fresh server fetch.

The core provider calls

before
refetching after mutations so the next call always hits the server.

children.label: onInvalidate
description:
Called by the core provider before an explicit refetch (e.g. after a
mutation) to clear the internal item cache. The next `findItems` call
will fetch from the server regardless of `searchQuery`.

children.label: onRefresh
description:
Re-runs the `decorate` callback on existing raw items without a
server round-trip. Call after external data mutations (e.g. star/unstar)
so the cached decorated items are refreshed.

children.label: getItems
description: Returns the full (unfiltered, decorated) item set from the most recent fetch.

interfaces.label: ContentEditorConfig
description:
Content editor configuration for `ContentListClientProvider`.

This is the Kibana-specific content editor integration. The base
`ContentListProvider` only knows about a simple `onInspect` callback;
this config produces that callback by wiring it to the Kibana content
editor flyout.

children.label: openContentEditor
description:
Callback to open the content editor flyout.
Obtained from `useOpenContentEditor()` in `@kbn/content-management-content-editor`.

children.label: isReadonly
description: Whether the content editor is readonly. Defaults to `false`.

children.label: customValidators
description: Custom validators for title/description fields.

children.label: onSave
description:
Called when the user saves metadata edits.
Receives the updated metadata fields. The callback should persist changes
and return a resolved promise on success.
signature: ((args: { id: string; title: string; description?: string | undefined; tags: string[]; }) => Promise<void>) | undefined

children.label: appendRows
description:
Optional render prop for additional rows in the content editor flyout.
Used by Dashboard to show content insights (panel counts, activity, etc.).

interfaces.label: ContentInsightsServiceOptions
description: Options for

children.label: http
description: Core HTTP service.

children.label: logger
description: Logger used for warnings on tracking failures.

children.label: domainId
description: Domain identifier (e.g. `'dashboard'`, `'visualization'`) used to scope insights.

interfaces.label: ContentListClientServices
description:
Services required by the client provider.

Extends the base `ContentListServices` with `uiSettings`, which is used to read
the `savedObjects:perPage` and `savedObjects:listingLimit` settings.

children.label: uiSettings
description: UI settings accessor. Required for reading defaults like page size and listing limit.

interfaces.label: DecorableFindItemsResult
description: Minimal shape consumed by

.

Defined locally rather than re-using `TableListViewFindItemsResult` so the
decorator can stay generic over the consumer's hit type without forcing
`TableListViewFindItemsResult` itself to become generic (which would be a
breaking change for existing consumers).

interfaces.label: DuplicateTitleValidatorOptions
description: Options for

children.label: findCurrentTitle
description:
Lookup callback returning the current saved title for the item identified
by `id`, or `undefined` if the item cannot be found / is in an error
state. Used as `lastSavedTitle` so the consumer's check can ignore
"duplicates" against the item being edited.

children.label: checkForDuplicate
description:
Returns `true` when the new title is valid (no duplicate exists). Throw
or resolve to `false` when a duplicate is detected.
signature: (args: { id: string; title: string; lastSavedTitle: string; }) => Promise<boolean | void>

children.label: getDuplicateTitleWarning
description:
Optional formatter for the warning message. Defaults to a generic
"Saving \"{value}\" creates a duplicate title." string.

interfaces.label: FavoritesServiceOptions
description: Options for

children.label: appId
description:
Application identifier reported to the favorites usage collector
(e.g. `'dashboards'`, `'visualize'`).

children.label: savedObjectType
description: Saved object type that owns the favorited items (e.g. `'dashboard'`).

children.label: http
description: Core HTTP service.

children.label: userProfile
description: Core user-profile service — required to gate per-user availability.

children.label: usageCollection
description: Optional usage collection start, used to record click events.

interfaces.label: PerformanceMetricsOptions
description: Options accepted by

children.label: analytics
description: Analytics service used to report the EBT event (typically `coreServices.analytics`).
signature: ) => void; reportEvent: <EventTypeData extends object>(eventType: string, eventData: EventTypeData) => void; readonly telemetryCounter$:

children.label: eventName
description:
EBT event name to report — see `SAVED_OBJECT_LOADED_TIME` /
`SAVED_OBJECT_DELETE_TIME` in consumers.

children.label: savedObjectType
description: Saved object type, recorded as `meta.saved_object_type`.

children.label: meta
description:
Extra metadata merged into the EBT event's `meta`. Useful for `total`
counts on bulk operations.
signature: ((args: unknown[], result: unknown) => Record<string, unknown>) | undefined

interfaces.label: RecentDecoration
description: Decoration added to each hit by

.
Consumers should extend their item type with these fields so
`Column.UpdatedAt`-style sort fields and `is:recent` filtering work
end-to-end.

- `recent` powers the toolbar's `is:recent` filter via the strategy's
  generic flag matcher (see `features.flags`).
- `accessedAt` is a numeric score (`history.length - index`) that can drive
  an opt-in "Recently viewed" sort field.

children.label: recent
description: True when the item appears in the recently-accessed history.

children.label: accessedAt
description:
Recency score — higher means more recently viewed. `0` for items not in
the history. A `desc` sort on this field mirrors the legacy
`sortByRecentlyAccessed` ordering.

interfaces.label: RecentlyAccessedDecoration
description: Result returned by

children.label: decorate
description:
Decorate a `findItems` result with `recent`/`accessedAt` based on the
supplied source. Reads the source synchronously at call time so each
fetch picks up the latest history.

children.label: flag
description:
Flag entry to add to `features.flags` so `EuiSearchBar` parses
`is:recent` into `filters.recent`.

children.label: RecentsFilter
description:
Declarative filter component for the toolbar's `<Filters>` slot. The
underlying renderer is closure-bound to the supplied source, so consumers
just `<recents.RecentsFilter />` without re-passing the service.

interfaces.label: RecentlyAccessedEntry
description:
Minimal entry shape needed to compute recency. Compatible with
`RecentlyAccessedHistoryItem` from `@kbn/recently-accessed`.

interfaces.label: RecentlyAccessedHistorySource
description:
Minimal source contract — anything that can return a most-recent-first
list of `{ id }` entries. Compatible with `RecentlyAccessed` from
`@kbn/recently-accessed` (consumers typically pass that directly).

children.label: get
description: Returns the current most-recent-first list of entries.

interfaces.label: RecentsFilterRendererProps
description: Props for

.

`EuiSearchBar`'s `custom_component` filters receive `query` and `onChange`.
The toolbar's `filter.createComponent` does not forward declarative
attributes, so the recently-accessed source and label are bound via
closure when the renderer is registered (see

children.label: query
description: Query object from `EuiSearchBar`.

children.label: onChange
description: `onChange` callback from `EuiSearchBar`.

children.label: service
description:
Recently-accessed source to read from. The renderer reads `.get()`
synchronously to determine whether to show itself.

children.label: label
description: Optional label override. Defaults to "Recent".

children.label: 'data-test-subj'
description: Optional `data-test-subj`.

interfaces.label: SavedObjectActivityRowProps
description: Props for

children.label: service
description: Content-insights client for this listing's domain (typically built with

children.label: item
description:
The item being inspected. The row reads `createdBy`, `createdAt`,
`updatedBy`, `updatedAt`, and `managed` for the activity card and uses
`id` to fetch view stats.

children.label: entityNamePlural
description: Plural entity label used by the activity card's "no creator/updater" tip.

interfaces.label: SavedObjectReference
description: Reference type matching `SavedObjectsFindOptionsReference` from Kibana core.

interfaces.label: TableListViewFindItemsOptions
description: Options passed to
alongside the search query.

children.label: references
description: Saved object references to filter by.

children.label: referencesToExclude
description: Saved object references to exclude.

children.label: listingLimit
description:
Maximum number of items to fetch from the server.

Populated automatically by

from the
`savedObjects:listingLimit` UI setting. Consumers should forward this value
to their underlying search call so they do not need to read the setting themselves.

interfaces.label: TableListViewFindItemsResult
description: Result type from the `TableListView` `findItems` function.

children.label: total
description: Total count of matching items.

children.label: hits
description: Items matching the search query.

interfaces.label: TagsApi
description:
Minimal structural type describing the part of `SavedObjectsTaggingApiUi`
needed to fill `services.tags`. Accepting a structural type rather than the
full plugin interface keeps the helper usable from any consumer that can
synthesize a `getTagList()` (e.g. Visualize's flattened tagging API).

children.label: getTagList
description: Synchronously return the list of available tags.

interfaces.label: TitleValidator
description:
Shape of a single entry in
`OpenContentEditorParams['customValidators']['title']`.

Re-declared locally to avoid importing the editor package's
`OpenContentEditorParams` (its `customValidators` is typed as a record of
arrays and would force the consumer to wrap this in another array).

misc.label: ContentListClientProviderProps
description:
Props for the Client provider.

This provider wraps an existing `TableListView`-style `findItems` function and handles
client-side sorting, filtering, and pagination.

misc.label: TableListViewFindItemsFn
description:
The existing `TableListView` `findItems` signature that consumers already have.

This matches the signature expected by `TableListViewTableProps.findItems`, with an
optional `signal` parameter for request cancellation.
